hero_product_name: the AnyWell YS16-1 PLUS electric wheelchair from the white-background reference photos
campaign_goal: Create three high-conversion 24-second Meta-style vertical ads for the AnyWell YS16-1 PLUS, emotionally inspired by the universal feeling of getting back outdoors near home.
target_audience: Heavy-set or plus-size American seniors age 60+, their spouses, and adult children buying mobility support for loved ones.
core_selling_points:
- now $599, reg. $999
- 500W dual motors for grass and gentle slopes
- 22-inch first-class wide seat
- supports up to 330 lbs
- folds in seconds for the trunk
use_scenarios:
- porch and finished ramp exit
- backyard path near trees
- quiet sunset lane near home
visual_style: Realistic, warm, outdoor, sales-driven, portrait-first American lifestyle advertising with strong product readability.
style_tone: persuasive, warm, calm, dignified, practical, never clinical
consistency_anchor: Match the real YS16-1 PLUS wheelchair from the white-background product photos while keeping it only in normal open riding position. Preserve the right-side joystick, wide seat feel, frame, footrest, wheel layout, and real product silhouette. Keep the rear/top-back area compact and proportional to the real chair: no extra rods, poles, antenna-like parts, cane-like extensions, or exaggerated push bars behind the backrest. If short integrated rear handles are naturally visible, keep them subtle, short, and close to the backrest. In every shot, keep the rider and wheelchair proportion close to c1 and c2: the rider naturally fills the broad seat, the chair reads substantial and premium, and neither subject looks oversized or undersized relative to the other.
additional_info: Hard Meta ad rules: heavy-set or plus-size American senior male rider age 60+ only, same adult female spouse across connected scenes, three shots at about 8 seconds each, seamless real-world B-roll only, no white-background cut-ins, and simple safe actions only. Use Jean's uploaded clip only as emotional reference for quiet freedom outdoors near home. Do not recreate a real person, real home, unfinished ramp, bare-tree woods layout, or testimonial comment. Do not place any readable ad copy, price tags, UI overlays, or gibberish text inside the generated footage itself. Keep each shot clean and reserve negative space only for optional post-production overlays added later.
music: Warm restrained cinematic underscore with light piano and a subtle modern pulse.

concepts.title: Nature Feels Close Again
concepts.core_theme: A husband returns to the backyard path while his wife follows with relief and warmth.
concepts.main_theme: Nature should not feel out of reach.
concepts.hook: Nature should not feel out of reach.
concepts.cta: Shop the AnyWell YS16-1 PLUS now.
concepts.visual_style: Warm morning backyard path near trees, spouse nearby, emotional but sales-ready.
concepts.continuity_rider_anchor: Keep the same heavy-set American senior husband age 60+ and the same wife across all three shots. He should visibly fill the wide seat naturally, with chair scale and framing similar to c1 and c2.
concepts.use_scenarios:
- back doorway or covered patio start
- garden path beside the home
- quiet residential sidewalk or lane
concepts.additional_info: This concept should feel closest to the universal emotional shape of Jean's clip, without matching the original house or woods. The route must clearly progress from the home exit to the garden to a quiet street-side moment so the three scenes feel like one believable outing, not three similar repeats.

shots.scene_description: A heavy-set white-haired American senior man in his 60s begins inside a bright back doorway or covered patio threshold, then calmly drives the YS16-1 PLUS out toward the garden while his wife opens space for him and follows with a relieved smile. The framing should feel like c1 and c2: medium-wide, the rider and chair dominate the portrait frame, the wide seat clearly supports his body, and the wheelchair looks full-sized and premium rather than tiny. The home exit, patio edge, and first movement outdoors should be clearly readable so this feels like the start of one continuous route. Leave clean upper-left or upper-right negative space, but keep the generated footage itself free of in-scene ad text or UI.
shots.camera_movement: smooth front-side push that begins near the doorway and travels outward toward the patio and garden
shots.lighting: soft morning daylight with warm highlights and natural depth
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium-wide composition, rider and chair large in frame, open back door or covered patio visible, generic home details only, no exact source-house cues
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair leaving the patio and moving toward the garden path
shots.voiceover_en: From the back door to the fresh morning air, the AnyWell YS16-1 PLUS helps him start the day feeling calm, supported, and ready to go farther.
shots.key_message: The outdoors can begin right at home.
shots.sfx: soft wheel roll, birds, and light morning ambience

shots.scene_description: The same husband now glides smoothly along a garden path beside the home, with flowers, lawn edges, or shrubs making it clear he has moved farther from the doorway and deeper into the yard. His right hand is clearly on the joystick while his wife walks a step behind, smiling. Keep the framing close to c1 and c2: the rider visibly fills the 22-inch seat naturally, thighs supported, torso proportionate to the backrest, and the chair remains large and balanced in frame. The environment should feel like the middle section of the same outing, not a repeated version of scene one. Reserve clean negative space only, and keep the generated footage itself free of in-scene ad text or UI.
shots.camera_movement: steady joystick-side front-profile tracking shot with subtle forward drift
shots.lighting: clean daylight filtered through trees with premium but realistic contrast
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium shot with rider and chair taking most of the frame height, garden path, lawn or shrubs, spouse softly visible, clear sense of leaving the house behind
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair reaching the far end of the garden route and naturally approaching a gate or sidewalk opening
shots.voiceover_en: Along the garden path, the wide twenty-two inch seat feels secure, and the smooth five-hundred watt dual motors make every move feel steady, easy, and controlled.
shots.key_message: Comfort and power become visible proof.
shots.sfx: smooth motor ambience, leaves, and light outdoor air

shots.scene_description: The same couple arrive at a quiet residential sidewalk or lane just beyond the garden, creating a clear final step in the route from home to outdoors. They slow into a warm lifestyle hero frame; he gives a small confident nod while seated naturally in the chair, still framed large like c1 and c2, and she walks beside him with visible relief. Keep soft background separation and enough negative space for optional post-production graphics later, while the generated footage itself stays free of in-scene ad text or UI.
shots.camera_movement: gentle moving settle from the sidewalk approach into a locked portrait hero frame
shots.lighting: warm flattering daylight with a polished direct-response finish
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait hero composition on a quiet residential sidewalk or lane, rider and chair prominent, open space reserved for trust and pricing graphics
shots.transition_anchor: end on a confident still frame for the close
shots.voiceover_en: And when the path opens past the garden, families get FDA-cleared safety, easy returns, and a price that makes freedom feel possible right now.
shots.key_message: Trust and price close the sale.
shots.sfx: music lift and soft outdoor ambience
